document.title: FCC Test Report
standards: FCC KDB 558074 D01 15.247 Meas Guidance v05r02

sourceSummary.oneLine: FCC Part 15.247 test report for Laird Connectivity 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n + Bluetooth 4.2 module models, with ST60-2230C selected as representative final test model.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports FCC compliance evidence for Bluetooth EDR/BR transmitter testing under 47 CFR FCC Part 15.247.
keyPoints: FCC ID is SQG-60SIPT for equipment described as an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n + Bluetooth 4.2 module.
Report FR740701-19AD Rev. 01 was issued as an initial issue on Nov. 28, 2023.
The tested sample was declared evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the stated standards.

Summary of test results lists Pass for conducted emissions, radiated emissions, band edge, conducted output power, number of hopping channels, hopping channel separation, dwell time, and antenna requirement.
Model ST60-2230C was selected as the representative model for final testing and only its data was recorded.

entities.name: ST60-2230C
entities.evidence: model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

concepts.name: Representative model testing
concepts.evidence: model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

concepts.name: Frequency hopping channel requirements
concepts.evidence: Frequency hopping systems in the 2400–2483.5 MHz band shall use at least 15 channels.

concepts.name: Dwell time limit
concepts.evidence: The average time of occupancy on any channel shall not be greater than 0.4 seconds within a period of 0.4 seconds multiplied by the number of hopping channels employed.

evidence.claim: The report number is FR740701-19AD and the report version is Rev. 01.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: FR740701-19AD Page : 1 of 60 Report Version: Rev. 01

evidence.claim: The document is an FCC Test Report for FCC ID SQG-60SIPT.
evidence.evidenceText: FCC Test Report FCC ID : SQG-60SIPT

evidence.claim: The equipment under test is described as an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n + Bluetooth 4.2 module.
The applicant is Laird Connectivity LLC.
The report applies the standard 47 CFR FCC Part 15.247.
The report release record lists Rev. 01 as the initial issue dated Nov. 28, 2023.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No. Version Description Issued Date FR740701-19AD Rev. 01 Initial issue Nov. 28, 2023

evidence.claim: The tested sample was evaluated and found in compliance with the requirements of the stated standards.
evidence.evidenceText: the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

evidence.claim: The report is a duplicate of original report FR740701AD with modifications changing brand name and applicant.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is issued as a duplicate report to original report no. FR740701AD. The modifications are changing brand name and applicant.

evidence.claim: Models ST60-SIPT, SU60-SIPT, ST60-2230C, and SU60-2230C are listed for the EUT.
evidence.evidenceText: The following models are provided to this EUT. Brand Name Model Name Product Name Description Laird Connectivity ST60-SIPT ... SU60-SIPT ST60-2230C ... SU60-2230C

evidence.claim: ST60-2230C was selected as the representative final test model.
evidence.evidenceText: The above models, model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

evidence.claim: The EUT Bluetooth frequency range is 2400-2483.5 MHz with channel frequencies 2402-2480 MHz and 79 channels.
Conducted output power passed with a reported power of 10.83 dBm under FCC rule 15.247(b)(1).
Conducted emissions, radiated emissions, band edge, conducted output power, hopping channels, channel separation, dwell time, and antenna requirement are reported as Pass.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of Test Results FCC Rules Test Items Measured Result ... Pass ... Pass ... Pass ... Pass ... Pass ... Pass ... Pass ... Pass

evidence.claim: The test laboratory used FCC Designation No. TW0009 and FCC site registration No. 207696.
evidence.evidenceText: FCC Designation No.: TW0009 FCC site registration No.: 207696

reviewFlags.reason: The report states it is a duplicate report to original report no. FR740701AD, with modifications changing brand name and applicant.
Some modulation text appears with OCR or character substitution issues, including variants such as л/4 QDPSK where the report elsewhere uses π/4-DQPSK.
